questions.question: Prove that the angle subtended by an arc at the center of a circle is twice the angle subtended at any point on the remaining part of the circle.
questions.answer: Using the properties of isosceles triangles formed by the radius and points on the circle, it can be shown that the central angle is twice any inscribed angle subtended by the same arc.
answer_outline: Identify points A, B on circle

questions.question: Prove that the opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°.
questions.answer: By joining the diagonal and using the angle subtended by the same arc, it can be shown that opposite angles sum to 180°.

questions.question: Prove that the tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal in length.
questions.answer: Using congruence of triangles formed by radii and tangents, it is shown that lengths from external point to points of contact are equal.
answer_outline: Draw circle with tangents PA and PB

questions.question: Prove that the radius of a circle is perpendicular to the tangent at the point of contact.
questions.answer: Using the property that the shortest distance from the center to a line is along the perpendicular, the radius meets the tangent at right angle.
answer_outline: Draw radius to point of contact

questions.question: Prove that the lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to two circles are equal.
questions.answer: Draw tangents from the external point to each circle and show triangles formed with centers are congruent, implying equal lengths.
answer_outline: Join centers to points of contact

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is cyclic if a pair of its opposite angles sum to 180°.
questions.answer: Using the converse of the inscribed angle theorem, a quadrilateral with opposite angles summing to 180° is cyclic.

questions.question: Prove that the angle between tangent and chord is equal to the angle in the alternate segment.
questions.answer: By drawing the radius to the point of contact and using triangle properties, the angle formed with the tangent equals the opposite inscribed angle.
answer_outline: Prove equality with alternate segment angle

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular drawn from the center of a circle to a chord bisects the chord.
questions.answer: Using triangle congruence formed by radii and chord segments, the perpendicular divides the chord equally.
answer_outline: Draw chord AB and perpendicular from O

questions.question: Prove that equal chords of a circle are equidistant from the center.
questions.answer: Draw perpendiculars from the center to each chord and use congruent triangles to show equal distances.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the midpoints of two parallel chords passes through the center of the circle.
questions.answer: Using coordinate method, midpoints lie on a line perpendicular to chords and passing through the center.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the angles subtended by two chords intersecting at a point inside the circle is 180°.
questions.answer: Use properties of cyclic quadrilaterals formed by the intersection point and endpoints of chords.

questions.question: Prove that the product of the segments of two intersecting chords is equal.
questions.answer: Let chords AB and CD intersect at P; then PA × PB = PC × PD using similar triangles.

questions.question: Prove that the tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Tangents at endpoints of diameter form right angles with radius and hence are parallel to each other.
answer_outline: Draw tangents at A and B

questions.question: Prove that a tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
questions.answer: By contradiction: assume not perpendicular and show distance from center to tangent is not minimum.
answer_outline: Draw radius to point of contact

questions.question: Prove that the angles in the same segment of a circle are equal.
questions.answer: Use isosceles triangles formed by radii to points on the segment and apply angle properties.

questions.question: Prove that the external angle formed by two intersecting tangents equals the angle subtended by the line joining points of contact at the center.
questions.answer: Use triangle formed by center and points of contact and tangent properties.
answer_outline: Draw tangents and points of contact

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the centers of two touching circles passes through the point of contact.
questions.answer: By definition of touching circles, distance between centers equals sum or difference of radii, lying on the line joining centers.
answer_outline: Identify centers and point of contact
Show line passes through contact point

questions.question: Prove that if two tangents intersect at an external point, the distances from the point to points of contact are equal.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by tangents and radii.
answer_outline: Show congruence and equality of lengths

questions.question: Prove that the angle subtended by a chord at the center is greater than the angle subtended on the remaining part of the circle.
questions.answer: Use isosceles triangle property and angle sum in triangle to show central angle > inscribed angle.

questions.question: Prove that the tangents drawn at the ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Tangents at endpoints form right angles with radius; thus, they are parallel.
answer_outline: Draw tangents at A and B

questions.question: Prove that equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the center.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by radii and chord endpoints to show angles at the center are equal.

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular bisector of a chord passes through the center of the circle.
questions.answer: Use congruence of triangles formed by radii to chord endpoints and midpoint to show perpendicular bisector passes through center.

questions.question: Prove that the product of the lengths of segments of two intersecting chords is equal.
questions.answer: Use similarity of triangles formed by intersecting chords to show PA × PB = PC × PD.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the angles in the same segment of a circle is equal.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem to show equality of angles in the same segment.

questions.question: Prove that the radius drawn to the point of contact of a tangent is perpendicular to the tangent.
questions.answer: Using the property that the shortest distance from center to tangent is along the radius, the radius meets tangent at 90°.
answer_outline: Draw radius to point of contact
